sourceMovie.title: The Informant!
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Informant! (2009) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Steven Soderbergh's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Made
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Informant! clicked for you, American Made (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Informant!?
faq.answer: Try American Made, The Sting, Lucky Number Slevin—each shares DNA with The Informant! while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Informant!?
faq.answer: The Informant! (2009) is best described as Drama, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Informant! worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
